signals.headline: XRP ETF Inflows Accelerate to $41
signals.raw:
XRP ETF Inflows Accelerate to $41.64M Weekly Record | YES | HIGH confidence**
U.S. spot XRP ETFs recorded $41.64 million in net inflows for the week ending April 16—the largest single-week inflow in three months and a 254% jump from the prior week[2]. This represents institutional conviction arriving before a potential technical breakout.
matched_markets: XRP Up or Down - April 22, 3:40PM-3:45PM ET
Will WTI Crude Oil (WTI) hit (HIGH) $95 Week of April 20 2026?

signals.headline: XRP Completes Cup-and-Handle Pattern Setup | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Completes Cup-and-Handle Pattern Setup | YES | HIGH confidence**
The technical pattern shows a cup running from mid-March highs near $1.46, scooping to $1.20 lows in early April, and curving back to the $1.44 neckline, with the handle consolidating just below[2]. All four exponential moving averages have been swept upside and are stacked in bullish order below price[2].
matched_markets: XRP Up or Down - April 22, 3:40PM-3:45PM ET

signals.headline: XRP Ledger Launches Programmable Privacy Features | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
XRP Ledger Launches Programmable Privacy Features | YES | HIGH confidence**
XRPL became the first public blockchain to offer programmable privacy and compliance controls built into its core through a RISC-V zero-knowledge verifier[1]. Smart Escrows are planned for Q2 2026, followed by Smart Vaults enabling fully private financial systems[1].

signals.headline: Bitcoin Stability Supporting XRP Price Band | YES | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Bitcoin Stability Supporting XRP Price Band | YES | MED confidence**
Bitcoin price stability in final hours of April 18 directly supported XRP holding within the $1.40-$1.50 band[4].

signals.headline: Japanese Banks Complete XRP Cross-Border Settlement Pilot | UNCONFIRMED | MED confidence**
signals.raw:
Japanese Banks Complete XRP Cross-Border Settlement Pilot | UNCONFIRMED | MED confidence**
Reports claim Japanese banks completed a live pilot comparing XRP settlements to SWIFT for Japan-Southeast Asia remittances, allegedly settling transactions in under four seconds at 60% less cost, though CryptoSlate could not independently verify this[1].
